---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Mark Deming"
date: "3/26/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load(2026, week = 12)
pi <- tuesdata$pi_digits

pacman::p_load(tidyverse, ggthemes, sysfonts, showtext, ggridges, slider)
#str(pi)
```

Runs...

Distribution...

```{r}
# Simple distribution of digits
ggplot(pi, aes(x = digit)) +
  geom_density() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 9, 1)) 
```

Rolling distributions...

```{r}
# Define window for rolling sums
window_size <- 1000

# Define dummy columns
dummy_cols <- paste0("d_", 0:9)

# Create dummies
pi_roll <- pi |> 
  mutate(
    d_0 = as.integer(digit == 0),
    d_1 = as.integer(digit == 1),
    d_2 = as.integer(digit == 2),
    d_3 = as.integer(digit == 3),
    d_4 = as.integer(digit == 4),
    d_5 = as.integer(digit == 5),
    d_6 = as.integer(digit == 6),
    d_7 = as.integer(digit == 7),
    d_8 = as.integer(digit == 8),
    d_9 = as.integer(digit == 9)
  )

# For rolling windows of 1K rows, calculate proportion for each digit
pi_roll <- pi_roll |> 
   mutate(
    across(
      starts_with("d_"),
      ~ slide_dbl(.x, ~ sum(.x), .before = window_size - 1, .complete = TRUE) / window_size
    )
  )

# Convert to long format
pi_long <- pi_roll %>%
  select(digit_position, starts_with("d_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("d_"),
    names_to = "digit",
    values_to = "frequency"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    digit = as.integer(sub("d_", "", digit))
  )


# Ready to plot
ggplot(pi_long, aes(x = 1, y = frequency)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ digit, ncol = 5) +
  labs(
    title = "Rolling Frequencies of Digits in Pi",
    x = "Digit position",
    y = "Frequency in 1,000-digit window"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


pi_long_sample <- pi_long %>%
  filter(digit_position %% 100 == 0)

# Ready to plot
ggplot(pi_long_sample, aes(x = 1, y = frequency)) +
  geom_boxplot(linewidth = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ digit, ncol = 5) +
  labs(
    title = "Rolling Frequencies of Digits in Pi",
    x = "Digit position",
    y = "Frequency in 1,000-digit window"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Boxplots not much more informative. Maybe geom_pointrange for mean plus 95% CI and geom_point for min and max with labels. Keep in mind for later, since still probably not a very interesting plot. 

Maybe a heat map...

```{r}

ggplot(pi_long_sample, aes(x = digit_position, y = digit, fill = frequency)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(
    title = "Rolling Digit Frequencies (Heatmap)",
    x = "Position",
    y = "Digit"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

Same problem: pi behaves too well. Better to treat it as a sequence. Start by laying out a grid with the first 1,000 digits...

```{r}
N <- 2000
digits_per_row <- 50

pi_grid <- pi %>%
  slice(1:N) %>%
  mutate(
    row = (digit_position - 1) %/% digits_per_row,
    col = (digit_position - 1) %% digits_per_row,
    group10 = col %/% 10,
    digit_label = ifelse(digit_position == 1,
                         paste0(digit, "."),
                         as.character(digit)))
  

# plot
ggplot(pi_grid, aes(x = col, y = -row)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = digit_label),
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey50",
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = seq(-0.5, digits_per_row, by = 10),
    color = "grey90",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.2) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "The First 1,000 Digits of π"
  )
  

# parameters
N <- 1000

# collapse first N digits into a single string
pi_string <- pi %>%
  slice(1:N) %>%
  pull(digit) %>%
  paste0(collapse = "")


# Candidate dates / sequences to search

candidates <- c("0617",
                "0703",
                "0629",
                "0823",
                "1776",
                "2000",
                "1492",
                "1981",
                "2014",
                "2002",
                "23188",
                "1225")

# search for first occurrence of each candidate
results <- tibble(
  pattern = candidates,
  first_position = str_locate(pi_string, candidates)[, "start"]
) %>%
  mutate(
    found = !is.na(first_position)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(found), first_position)

results_found <- results %>%
  filter(found)

results_found

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)

highlight_df <- results_found %>%
  mutate(
    pattern_length = nchar(pattern),
    digit_position = map2(
      first_position,
      pattern_length,
      ~ seq(.x, .x + .y - 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  unnest(digit_position)

digits_per_row <- 50

pi_grid <- pi %>%
  slice(1:N) %>%
  mutate(
    row = (digit_position - 1) %/% digits_per_row,
    col = (digit_position - 1) %% digits_per_row,
    digit_label = ifelse(
      digit_position == 1,
      paste0(digit, "."),
      as.character(digit)
    )
  )


pi_grid_highlight <- pi_grid %>%
  left_join(
    highlight_df %>% select(digit_position, pattern),
    by = "digit_position"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    highlighted = !is.na(pattern)
  )

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pi_grid_highlight, aes(x = col, y = -row)) +
  geom_tile(
    data = pi_grid_highlight %>% filter(highlighted),
    aes(fill = pattern),
    width = 0.95,
    height = 0.95,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = digit_label),
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey35",
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = seq(-0.5, digits_per_row, by = 10),
    color = "grey90",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "0617" = "#F4A7A3",
      "2000" = "#A7C7E7",
      "1492" = "#C8DDAF",
      "1776" = "#F2C38F",
      "1914" = "#D6B3E6",
      "1945" = "#F6D6A8",
      "1989" = "#A9D6C3",
      "2001" = "#E7B3C8",
      "2020" = "#D0D0D0"
    ),
    na.translate = FALSE
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.2) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "The First 1,000 Digits of π",
    subtitle = "Selected sequences highlighted",
    fill = "Sequence"
  )



```
